Understanding Termite Damage and Protection

Jennifer Reyes — March 5, 2026

Termites cause billions of dollars in property damage each year, often going undetected until significant harm has occurred. Understanding how to identify termite damage and protect your property is essential for every homeowner.

Types of Termites

The Philippines is home to several termite species, with subterranean termites being the most common and destructive. These termites live underground and build mud tubes to access wood above ground. Drywood termites infest dry wood and don't require contact with soil. Understanding the type of termite is crucial for effective treatment.

Signs of Termite Damage

Look for hollow-sounding wood when tapped, visible mud tubes on exterior walls or foundations, and small piles of wing debris near windows or doors. You might also notice blistering or darkening of wood surfaces, stuck windows or doors due to moisture from termite activity, and frass (termite droppings) that looks like small wood-colored pellets.

The Cost of Termite Damage

Termite damage is not typically covered by homeowners insurance, making prevention and early detection crucial. A severe infestation can compromise structural integrity, leading to repairs costing thousands or even hundreds of thousands of pesos. Regular inspections and preventive treatments are far more cost-effective than extensive repairs.

Professional Treatment Options

Professional termite control includes chemical barriers, baiting systems, and wood treatments. Pre-construction treatments create a protective barrier before building, while post-construction treatments address existing structures. Our chemical proofness service provides long-lasting protection using eco-friendly formulations that are safe for your family and the environment.

Prevention Strategies

Eliminate moisture sources around your foundation, maintain proper ventilation in crawl spaces, remove wood debris and stumps from your property, and ensure wood structures don't have direct contact with soil. Regular professional inspections can detect termite activity before significant damage occurs.

Protecting your property from termite damage requires vigilance and professional expertise. QUREIT Pest Control offers comprehensive termite inspection and treatment services, including our industry-leading chemical proofness treatment. Don't wait until you see signs of damage—schedule a termite inspection today to ensure your property is protected.
